purpose: Defines the !scaffold slash command — generates a CLAUDE.md for a project that lacks one by scanning structure, detecting tech stack, and reading existing docs. Six-step workflow: validate target (refuse if CLAUDE.md exists without --force; --force requires confirmation), detect tech stack from package.json/tsconfig/next.config/firebase.json/requirements.txt/go.mod/Cargo.toml/etc., scan directory structure (top-level + one level into src/app/lib/functions), read existing docs (README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/, .env.example, _commands/), generate CLAUDE.md with Tech Stack/Architecture/File Structure/Conventions/Commands/Environment sections, write file and report. DESCRIPTIVE not prescriptive — documents what IS, not what SHOULD BE. Marks unknowns as TODO rather than guessing.
gotchas: Differentiation from !create_claude is critical: !scaffold DOCUMENTS what IS; !create_claude PRESCRIBES what SHOULD BE.
NEVER overwrite existing CLAUDE.md without --force — existing files represent manual user work.
--force still requires confirmation prompt before overwrite.
Mark unknowns as TODO — never guess at architecture/conventions that can't be inferred from filesystem. TODO > wrong assumption.
Read ONLY structure and config files — never deep-read source code. Scaffold reads: configs, READMEs, directory listings, command files.
Generated files are STARTING POINTS — output must explicitly tell the user to review and expand TODO sections.
patterns: Conservative inference: prefer TODO markers over guessed values.
conventions: Modifies filesystem (writes new CLAUDE.md only when none exists, or with --force).
Section structure: Tech Stack / Architecture / File Structure / Conventions / Commands / Environment.
